description_long: This object is a military-style jet airplane with a slender fuselage and sharply pointed nose, finished in a mottled camouflage pattern. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, extending laterally with a slight sweep and no visible winglets. The tail section features a prominent single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail design. The landing gear is visible and appears to be a tricycle configuration, with two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel. The cockpit is positioned at the forward section of the fuselage and is enclosed, likely designed for a single pilot. The engine is mounted at the rear of the fuselage, with an exhaust nozzle visible, indicating a single jet engine setup.
description: A camouflaged, military-style jet airplane with a pointed nose, mid-mounted swept wings, a single rear jet engine, and a conventional tail. The enclosed cockpit is forward-mounted, and the landing gear is in a tricycle configuration.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the camouflage pattern, straight mid-mounted wings, and the pointed nose.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and the jet engine exhaust at the tail.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view displaying the landing gear arrangement and the underbelly details of the fuselage.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the pointed nose, cockpit canopy, and the symmetry of the wings and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight, slightly swept wings and no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main units and a single nose gear, all fixed and visible
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy
